name: Pillow Case Jerky
recipeInstructions:
1. Obtain as large a boneless butt roast as possible.
2. Cut the individual muscles out of the meat and remove all fat and connecting tissue.
3. Slice the meat across the grain to thin pieces, ideally between 1/16 and 1/8 inch thick.
4. Season both sides of the meat generously with salt and pepper (more pepper than salt).
5. Arrange the meat strips on a cake rack or oven rack without overlapping them.
6. Place the rack in an insert for a fireplace (smoker) and cook at low heat for 8 to 10 hours, turning every 3 to 4 hours until fully dried.
7. Alternatively, use a dehydrator or oven, but note that the result may not be as good.
8. Store the jerky in a pillowcase; avoid storing it in plastic, as the meat needs to breathe, and a paper bag will impart a paper taste.
From "Cow Country Cusine" by Kathy G. Mc Craine
